pi_items.source: Verified by testing against actual test data in setup_task.sh. Pattern matches all 15 real SSNs in format XXX-XX-XXXX with word boundaries.
Verified by testing against actual test data in setup_task.sh. Pattern matches all 10 real MRNs in format MRN-XXXXXX.
Confirmed in README.md created by setup_task.sh (lines 125): 'Use placeholders XXX-XX-XXXX'
Confirmed in README.md created by setup_task.sh (lines 125): 'Use placeholders ... MRN-000000'

Verified by summing setup_task.sh comments: File A(5) + File B(3) + File C(4) + File D(3) = 15. Note: TestPatient.java contains a 13th SSN marked 'Dummy' (000-00-0000) which is explicitly excluded from the 5-count for that file.
Verified by summing setup_task.sh comments: File A(3) + File B(3) + File C(2) + File D(2) = 10. Note: README.md contains 'MRN-000000' which is the placeholder/replacement string, not real PII data.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves redacting PII from a synthetic PatientConnector project containing 15 Social Security Numbers in the format XXX-XX-XXXX (3-2-4 digits with hyphens) and 10 Medical Record Numbers in the format MRN-XXXXXX (6 digits). All PII values are test data created programmatically by the setup script, not from real patient records. The project directory is /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/PatientConnector. The regex patterns provided (\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b for SSN and MRN-\d{6} for MRN) correctly match all instances. Placeholder replacements are XXX-XX-XXXX and MRN-000000 respectively.
